strengths: Proven track record in building event-driven, distributed systems (e.g., GISCollective platform, real-time meta data handling at Dunnhumby)
Hands-on experience with AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling (MCP, agentic workflows at re:cinq)
Strong engineering leadership and mentorship (e.g., organizing refactoring dojos, Selenium courses, CI/CD practices)
concerns: Limited explicit experience with fintech infrastructure (credit systems, payment flows, embedded finance) or regulatory compliance in financial contexts
No demonstrated production experience with multi-agent AI systems in high-stakes commercial environments (resume highlights prototyping and tooling rather than scaled deployments)
Scant evidence of scaling engineering teams beyond 10-15 people or operating in venture-backed scale-up environments
response.justification: Bogdan demonstrates strong technical depth in distributed systems, event-driven architectures, and emerging AI-agent tooling, which aligns well with the architectural demands of agentic AI. However, his resume lacks direct fintech infrastructure experience (e.g., credit risk, real-time trading systems) and evidence of scaling engineering teams to 30+ people in regulated, high-stakes environments. His recent role at re:cinq suggests growing engagement with agentic workflows, but this is not yet reflected in large-scale fintech deployments.
